entries.text: projectName: Gladdis
added_openai_models: {"models":["gpt-4o","gpt-4o-mini","o1","o3-mini","gpt-4.1 mini"]}

value.review: Grok provider uses raw /chat/completions. Has reasoning_effort, cached token parsing, history via recall. No compaction. turnContextPolicy and historyTools handle selective context.
optimizations: Add context compaction step before long histories sent to Grok (official docs recommendation)
Leverage existing recall/summary for opaque reusable summaries
Ensure high cachedInputTokens by stable prompts
Tighten caps on tool results for Grok turns

entries.text: grok_optimization_plan: {"review":"Grok provider uses raw /chat/completions. Has reasoning_effort, cached token parsing, history via recall. No compaction. turnContextPolicy and historyTools handle selective context.","optimizations":["Add context compaction step before long histories sent to Grok (official docs recommendation)","Leverage existing recall/summary for opaque reusable summaries","Ensure high cachedInputTokens by stable prompts","Tighten caps on tool results for Grok turns"]}
For provider-specific dreaming/modal changes, keep any human-readable “Review summary” deterministic and derived from existing DreamDiff/adoption/diff data rather than generated by another model.

evidence.turnExcerpt: I would keep the summary deterministic and derived from the existing `DreamDiff`, not generated by another model.

entries.text: Gladdis’s “External Chrome/Chromium commands bypass Gladdis's embedded browser” warning can be triggered by the search text itself, so avoid matching the forbidden phrase in repo/tool commands and patch the parser accordingly.

evidence.turnExcerpt: The command policy is catching the *text being searched for*, not just commands being run.
we keep getting this failed tool ... Error External Chrome/Chromium commands bypass...
External Chrome/Chromium commands bypass Gladdis's embedded browser. Use the gladdis dynamic tools...
Yep, that confirms the false positive. The command policy is catching the *text being searched for*...
fix it so it stops happening "⚠️ External Chrome/Chromium commands bypass..."
Yep, that confirms the false positive. The command policy is catching the *text being searched for*

entries.text: Keep dreaming’s UI/action flow review-first by default (do not auto-apply/diff results without an explicit adopt/discard step).

evidence.turnExcerpt: The memory/dream flow is intentionally review-first and never auto-applies, per `MemoryButton.tsx`
I’m going to make the summary expand naturally... add wrapping guards...
I’m going to add a persisted adoption policy to the diff and make adopt require a clean review record...
ok this is better -- now in the modal it has the adopt button which doesnt seem to be functional...

entries.text: Dream adoption should be blocked by persisted, user-visible verification/diff issues (low confidence, missing evidence, unsupported/partial verification), with the Adopt button disabled and Dreamer.adopt enforcing the same policy from disk.

evidence.turnExcerpt: add a persisted adoption policy ... make adopt require a clean review record before promoting
Focused dreaming tests are green ... add a disk-level adoption test ... Dreamer.adopt() enforces it
The live pending diff is blocked by a single `partial-verification` issue.

entries.text: To test repo-audit accuracy and grounding, run paired audits with semantically close natural-language prompts (inefficient → efficient) in a fresh chat, then verify the second answer searches genuinely different evidence.

evidence.turnExcerpt: Those prompts are useful ... broad, natural, and semantically close ... The second answer should not just negate

entries.text: When testing folder-scoped repo search performance/caching, use a sequence of natural-language “Search only in <folder>” prompts across the same folder, then repeat with a different folder to confirm cache scoping isolation.

evidence.turnExcerpt: Use prompts like ... Search only in src/main ... Then test scope isolation: Search only in src/renderer...

entries.text: The system-prompt assembly in `src/main/models/prompts.ts` was refactored into an immutable static base plus a cached per-turn delta, and the guidance delta is now selected via cached granular blocks keyed by a bitmask capability signature.

evidence.turnExcerpt: Split system prompt base and delta... buildStaticAgentBase(signature)... per-turn delta
Introduced `GUIDANCE_BLOCKS`... `guidanceKey(tools)` so guidance caching is keyed...
Switched `guidanceKey` to a bitmask-based capability signature.
split the system prompt into a truly immutable base and a small per turn delta
Switched `guidanceKey` to a bitmask-based capability signature... agentGuidanceForTools uses ... cache lookup

entries.text: To reduce tool payload/tokens, avoid escalating to the `full` agent tool profile for mixed-intent prompts; instead prefer a lean starter profile and allow explicit `request_tools` with a `tools` array, then cache resolved tool lists via normalized grant names.

evidence.turnExcerpt: Made mixed-intent prompts avoid defaulting to `full`... prefers a lean starter profile...
Added normalization + caching layer... turn-level tool-list cache (`TOOL_LIST_CACHE`)...

entries.text: `audit_codebase` over-triggering was mitigated by softening the tool description to remove “Call this first” language and adding runtime intent gating so the tool only dispatches when user intent explicitly matches audit wording.

evidence.turnExcerpt: Softened tool prompt language... Removed “Call this first…” guidance.
Added runtime gating... explicit intent detection before dispatching `audit_codebase`.

entries.text: The memory/dream review summary modal clipping was caused by a nested scroll cap on `.dream-review-summary` (fixed at ~220px), and the fix was to remove the fixed nested scroll so the modal’s single scroll area owns scrolling.

evidence.turnExcerpt: The clipping is coming from an inner scroll box on `.dream-review-summary`: it caps... 220px
I’m going to make the summary expand naturally... and add wrapping guards

entries.text: In the current dreaming/memory adopt UX, a successful adopt updates the button label to “Already adopted” and shows “Memory adopted.” but does not auto-dismiss/close the modal because there is no timer and it doesn’t call `setDiff(null)`.

evidence.turnExcerpt: No. In the current code, after a successful adopt it does this... does not call setDiff(null) and there is no setTimeout
After a successful adopt, `MemoryButton.adopt()` only does `setDiff({... awaitingAdopt: false})`

entries.text: Browser tooling discovery/action hierarchy was updated to keep `grep_click` and `grep_type` as primary action tools while demoting older action tools, and the Codex dynamic browser tool lists/prompts were updated to include the new preferred tools.

evidence.turnExcerpt: Kept `grep_click` and `grep_type` prominent... Updated the browser system prompt to treat them as PRIMARY action tools
Codex browser tool list now includes: grep_click grep_type ... updated ... instructions

entries.text: To fix “memory/dream review summary” clipping, remove the nested scroll cap on the summary container and instead let wrapping/height be governed by the modal’s single scroll area.

evidence.turnExcerpt: remove the fixed nested scroll, add wrapping guards. no fixed internal height, no nested scroll

value.task: Review current Agents header dropdown and create agent feature with optimize testing

current_workflow.step_3: User writes brief description of agent type
current_workflow.step_5: System creates optimized agent based on selected mode
current_workflow.goal: Test and calibrate quality levels between quick and deep optimize

ui_components.header: AgentPicker.tsx - shows current agents in dropdown
ui_components.modal: AgentBuilderModal.tsx - 666 lines, handles entire create/edit flow
ui_components.optimize_button: Two modes: 'Quick' and 'Deep' toggle in modal

quick_mode_behavior.description: Quick mode runs a compact workspace summary only
steps: 1. Calls repoIntelligence.repoOverview() with workspace root and rough prompt focus
2. Returns quick summary in format: 'Mode: quick' + overview summary
3. Fallback: if overview fails, returns focus text only

deep_mode_behavior.description: Deep mode runs search, read spans, and dossier synthesis
steps: 2. Extract structured data: package name, package manager, scripts
4. Collect top 6 results per query, suggest spans for reading
5. Read up to 4 suggested code spans (2 per query max)
6. researchDossier.researchDossier() - synthesis pass with up to 12 results
7. Combine all evidence into detailed context summary
deep_mode_behavior.token_efficiency: Much heavier - runs multiple searches, reads files, synthesizes

what_optimizer_does_with_evidence.system_prompt: Gladdis Agent Builder - expert designer of compact, high-leverage agents
what_optimizer_does_with_evidence.input_to_llm: JSON containing: roughGoal, requestedName, existingAgent (if editing), selectedModel, optimizationMode, workspaceEvidence
what_optimizer_does_with_evidence.llm_task: Convert rough goal into saved agent definition with optimized system prompt

output_schema.name: short agent name
output_schema.preferredTools: list of tools agent should prefer
output_schema.disallowedTools: list of tools agent should avoid

design_priorities: Optimize for direct task completion at highest practical quality
Minimize token use by front-loading durable context, constraints, exact targets, decision rules
Do not rediscover info already in workspace evidence or user goal
Treat exact paths, component names, commands, schemas, APIs as valuable context
Keep agent general enough for task family; don't hard-code unless needed
Preserve user intent - don't invent facts or requirements

current_quality_testing.what_varies: Amount and depth of workspace evidence fed to optimizer
current_quality_testing.quick: Only repoOverview() - basic structure and focus
current_quality_testing.deep: Overview + 3 search queries + file read spans + research dossier
current_quality_testing.goal: Understand where quality levels currently are and find sweet spot
current_quality_testing.hypothetical_measurement: Agent prompt quality, specificity, tool selection, workflow correctness

fallback_behavior.deep_mode_fallback: If deep fails, automatically falls back to quick + error message
fallback_behavior.complete_failure: If no workspace selected, uses portable prompt with goal only

existing_surfaces: Electron desktop app with main/preload/renderer split; no PWA manifest/service worker/Vite PWA plugin in package or source searches
Renderer depends on window.gladdis preload IPC API
Existing localhost HTTP surface is Claude Code MCP bridge bound to 127.0.0.1 with bearer token; not user-facing phone API
Remote debugging can expose CDP only when opt-in but is loopback-only
Renderer layout is desktop split-pane with native WebContentsView overlay tracked by DOM slot bounds
value.recommended_direction: Implement local companion daemon/web gateway first for phone chat/control, not full PWA port. Use shared GladdisApi/IPCs as contract, start with chat/conversation/workspace/status endpoints and WebSocket/SSE streams; keep browser page rendering via screenshots or simplified remote controls. Full PWA requires replacing Electron-only browser/terminal/file features or a server backend facade.

entries.text: findings: {"status":"synthesis_ready","existing_surfaces":["Electron desktop app with main/preload/renderer split; no PWA manifest/service worker/Vite PWA plugin in package or source searches","Renderer depends on window.gladdis preload IPC API","Existing localhost HTTP surface is Claude Code MCP bridge bound to 127.0.0.1 with bearer token; not user-facing phone API","Remote debugging can expose CDP only when opt-in but is loopback-only","Renderer layout is desktop split-pane with native WebContentsView overlay tracked by DOM slot bounds"],"recommended_direction":"Implement local companion daemon/web gateway first for phone chat/control, not full PWA port. Use shared GladdisApi/IPCs as contract, start with chat/conversation/workspace/status endpoints and WebSocket/SSE streams; keep browser page rendering via screenshots or simplified remote controls. Full PWA requires replacing Electron-only browser/terminal/file features or a server backend facade."}

value.clarified_goal: Phone is only a remote chat input/output surface. A phone message should enter the same main-process chat path as the desktop composer and streamed response events should be piped back to phone.
evidence: Desktop send path: src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx constructs ChatRequest then calls window.gladdis.chat.send
Preload exposes chat.send on GladdisApi in shared/api.ts and src/preload/index.ts
Main IPC handler src/main/index.ts calls registry.chat.send(req), nudges autoDream, and prewarms Cursor bridge for Cursor models
ServiceRegistry constructs ChatService with stream callback using chatStreamBatcher
ChatService.send is the lifecycle owner and already emits ChatStreamEvent typed in shared/chat.ts
Existing ChatRequest and ChatStreamEvent types are sufficient for HTTP/WebSocket bridge
value.recommendation: Build an opt-in authenticated RemoteChatServer/PhoneBridge inside main that calls registry.chat.send(req) and subscribes to same stream emitter. Do not build PWA first; do not expose CDP/terminal/filesystem broadly.

entries.text: clarified_phone_chat_bridge: {"status":"review_complete","clarified_goal":"Phone is only a remote chat input/output surface. A phone message should enter the same main-process chat path as the desktop composer and streamed response events should be piped back to phone.","evidence":["Desktop send path: src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx constructs ChatRequest then calls window.gladdis.chat.send","Preload exposes chat.send on GladdisApi in shared/api.ts and src/preload/index.ts","Main IPC handler src/main/index.ts calls registry.chat.send(req), nudges autoDream, and prewarms Cursor bridge for Cursor models","ServiceRegistry constructs ChatService with stream callback using chatStreamBatcher","ChatService.send is the lifecycle owner and already emits ChatStreamEvent typed in shared/chat.ts","Existing ChatRequest and ChatStreamEvent types are sufficient for HTTP/WebSocket bridge"],"recommendation":"Build an opt-in authenticated RemoteChatServer/PhoneBridge inside main that calls registry.chat.send(req) and subscribes to same stream emitter. Do not build PWA first; do not expose CDP/terminal/filesystem broadly."}

value.answer_summary: PWA is not wrong as a phone UI shell, but wrong as primary implementation path for Gladdis because the actual app runtime depends on Electron main, preload IPC, WebContentsView/CDP, filesystem/shell/tool access, and local stores. For clarified goal, build an opt-in authenticated phone bridge in main process that serves a tiny installable web UI and calls registry.chat.send, streaming ChatStreamEvent back to phone. A PWA can sit on top of that bridge later for home-screen/offline shell benefits.
evidence: src/main/index.ts uses Electron BaseWindow/WebContentsView/ipcMain and routes CHAT_SEND to registry.chat.send
src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx builds ChatRequest and calls window.gladdis.chat.send
ServiceRegistry wires ChatService stream callback to chatStreamBatcher
shared/chat.ts already defines ChatRequest and ChatStreamEvent
PWA artifact search found no app manifest/service worker/vite-pwa/workbox implementation

For clarified goal, build an opt-in authenticated phone bridge in main process that serves a tiny installable web UI and calls registry.chat.send, streaming ChatStreamEvent back to phone. A PWA can sit on top of that bridge later for home-screen/offline shell benefits.","evidence":["src/main/index.ts uses Electron BaseWindow/WebContentsView/ipcMain and routes CHAT_SEND to registry.chat.send","src/preload/index.ts exposes window.gladdis via ipcRenderer","src/renderer/components/ChatPanel.tsx builds ChatRequest and calls window.gladdis.chat.send","ServiceRegistry wires ChatService stream callback to chatStreamBatcher","shared/chat.ts already defines ChatRequest and ChatStreamEvent","PWA artifact search found no app manifest/service worker/vite-pwa/workbox implementation"]}

value.answer_summary: PWA is likely best first client because it installs from landing page without app stores. For anywhere access while desktop is on, use desktop daemon outbound-connected to a cloud relay, with phone PWA connecting to relay. If computer is truly off, local Gladdis cannot act; options are queued messages, Wake-on-LAN/sleep wake, or a hosted/headless Gladdis/cloud agent with synced state and permissions. True 'acts as user at the computer while computer is off' requires another always-on compute environment.

entries.text: anywhere_phone_options: {"status":"completed","answer_summary":"PWA is likely best first client because it installs from landing page without app stores. For anywhere access while desktop is on, use desktop daemon outbound-connected to a cloud relay, with phone PWA connecting to relay. If computer is truly off, local Gladdis cannot act; options are queued messages, Wake-on-LAN/sleep wake, or a hosted/headless Gladdis/cloud agent with synced state and permissions. True 'acts as user at the computer while computer is off' requires another always-on compute environment."}

summary: Added opt-in RemoteChatServer with bearer-token auth, SSE stream endpoint, chat send/abort APIs, conversation/model APIs, and a minimal installable PWA shell served at /app.
Added ServiceRegistry chat stream subscription fan-out so remote clients can receive ChatStreamEvent without bypassing existing renderer batching.
Wired server startup behind GLADDIS_PHONE_BRIDGE=1 with host/port/token/CORS env vars.
Added unit tests for unauthorized send rejection and authenticated remote turn persistence/stream delta handling.
validation: npm run check passed: 75 test files, 585 passing, 9 skipped
notes: Worktree still contains many unrelated pre-existing unstaged changes; commit staged only the bridge scaffold.

Refactored RemoteChatServer startup so the daemon can be started/stopped from UI, with status exposed to renderer.
Added Chat settings Phone tab with Start local, Start LAN, Copy URL, Stop, and Refresh controls.
LAN mode now advertises a reachable IPv4 LAN address instead of 0.0.0.0 when possible.

value.question: How do we build phone/PWA access extremely well with speed as first-class?
basis: Current scaffold is direct main-process phone bridge with SSE streaming and PWA shell
Future anywhere mode likely needs outbound desktop-to-relay connection plus phone-to-relay connection
User cares about phone as fast chat remote, not app-store native app

summary: Added `ws`-based persistent transport to the local phone bridge with a small command/event protocol.
RemoteChatServer now accepts `/ws` upgrades, sends `ready` and `ack` events, and broadcasts chat stream events over websocket alongside SSE.
The installable phone web client now uses websocket send/stream/reconnect behavior instead of separate EventSource + POST flow.
validation: npm run check passed: 76 test files, 589 passing, 9 skipped
npm run build passed with the existing memoryStore chunk warning
notes: Visual confirmation of the phone UI inside the running app was not conclusive from the app-level screenshot alone.

summary: Added client message ids to the phone socket protocol and explicit `status` events for connection state.
RemoteChatServer now dedupes websocket sends by client token + clientMessageId, including concurrent retries, so reconnects do not create duplicate desktop turns.
The installable phone client now keeps an outbox, retries unacked sends after reconnect, and routes streamed assistant output back into the correct placeholder bubble.
validation: npm run check passed: 76 test files, 590 passing, 9 skipped
npm run build passed with the existing memoryStore chunk warning

summary: Added a persisted PhoneSessionStateStore so each paired phone can recover its active conversation and in-flight turn state after reloads.
RemoteChatServer now authenticates websocket clients into durable session keys, sends a session snapshot on `ready`, and updates/clears pending turn state during the chat stream lifecycle.

The remote phone app now persists its local outbox in localStorage, restores the saved conversation from the desktop daemon, reconciles local pending messages with server session state, and safely retries after reconnect.
validation: npm run check passed: 77 test files, 593 passing, 9 skipped
npm run build passed with the existing memoryStore chunk warning
notes: Committed without staging unrelated user changes in src/main/index.ts; the persisted-session wiring there was staged selectively.
I visually confirmed the Gladdis app window was running, but I did not fully drive the served remote phone page in-browser during this slice.
